        "leave from useless groups → unfollow useless groups",
        "at VK → on VK",

        "write me at personal messages → message me"

Focus on improving preposition usage and sentence variety to progress."
}

        "very important thing → a very important thing"
    ],

While there are grammatical errors (e.g., word order, prepositions) and some awkward phrasing, they don't impede overall comprehension. To improve, focus on preposition usage ('how to organize' vs. 'options how') and article placement ('a very important thing'). The text shows clear rhetorical organization and attempt at idiomatic expressions, which justifies a B2 rating."
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B2",

    "corrections": [
        "easily → easy",

        "exam → exams",

        "some → some (contextually unclear, consider 'some tips')",
        "for became diffiecult study easilier → to make difficult study easier",
        "rewrite → copy (more natural phrasing)",
        "solution → solutions",
        "write → write (no change needed, but contextually better as 'copy the solution')",
        "exmaple → example",
        "football → football (no change needed, but punctuation issue with 'football your brain' → 'football. Your brain')",
        "relax and do → relaxes and does",
        "solve spend → spend less time solving",
        "time for doing → time to do",
        "traning → training",
        "time of sleeping → more sleep",
        "aproximately → approximately",
        "of course → of course (punctuation issue: 'of course, you do not...')",
        "for working → for work",
        "everything → everything (contextually better as 'anything')",
        "getting → getting (grammar issue: 'Do homework after getting' → 'Do homework after getting done')",
        "dont → don't",
        "better mark → better marks",
        "esalier → easier",
        "Well, → Well, (discourse marker issue: 'Well, It was four tips' → 'Well, these four tips')"
    ],
    "feedback": "The text shows B2-level organization with clear tips, but frequent grammar and lexical errors hinder clarity. Complex sentences like 'If you sleep more than six hours, you wake up and understand that you can do everything' demonstrate attempt at sophistication. However, issues with verb tenses, prepositions, and word choice (e.g., 'for became diffiecult study easilier') reduce effectiveness. Strengthening grammar accuracy and natural phrasing will elevate the text."
}
